source.title: Supreme Court weighs 'geofence warrants' for cellphone data

source_text:
Source type: news_article_url
URL: https://abcnews.com/Politics/supreme-court-weighs-geofence-warrants-cellphone-data/story?id=132358022&cid=social_twitter_wt
Title: "Supreme Court weighs 'geofence warrants' for cellphone data"
Published: 2026-04-27
Excerpt: "The Supreme Court on Monday is considering whether investigators can also use so-called 'geofence warrants' to do the reverse -- scanning cell phone data of thousands of innocent individuals in hopes of finding a suspect to apprehend. The landmark case is the first time the justices will consider whether the controversial practice of digital dragnets... violate constitutional protections against unreasonable searches."
Accessed: 2026-04-27
Context: Chatrie v. U.S., involving 4th Amendment challenge to geofence warrants. Petitioner Okello Chatrie indicted for armed robbery; authorities used Google location data under a geofence warrant. Decision expected by end of June.
notes: This R2 pins a source from Issue #3, but the source is about geofence warrants/Chatrie v. U.S. It does not verify the MN_JUDICIARY_001 passport/bail-flight claim. Case proof remains pending.
